evidence_quotes.quote: Games which today cost EUR 80 a day cannot help with this.
Examples: I bought the crew several years ago. This can only be played (singleplayer) or with friends (multiplayer)
Some months ago Ubisoft decided to connect the servers and report to the players that this game has come to an end and is taken from your game list.
This effectively obliges the consumer to buy the next game like a crew 2, which in turn costs around EUR 50a 80.

paragraphs.text: Good Day, I strongly support the stop killing game initiative. It has already collected more than 1.3 million signatures, so it says something about this initaive. The reason for writing this is because I am not a rich citizen who can buy everything. I often have to make choices about what I buy or not. Games which today cost EUR 80 a day cannot help with this. Should I nevertheless choose to buy one of these games, it is a fine thoughts to know that this game remains with me and that big companies such as Ubisoft and Electronic Arts cannot ‘repackage’ them from me Natural, it is unrealistic to ask whether they will update, maintain and hold a game for all, but this is not what I ask. Examples: I bought the crew several years ago. This can only be played (singleplayer) or with friends (multiplayer) Some months ago Ubisoft decided to connect the servers and report to the players that this game has come to an end and is taken from your game list. This is absurt! You could compare it with a bread what you buy. If it is about the shelf-life date, it is not discarded by the supermarket either. You first look at whether it is still good and then decide if you eat it or discard it. This should also be the case with video games. You decide whether you still want to play the game or not, even if this may be more difficult with friends, because the creator of the game decides to close the servers (and again that servers are closed is entirely real). This effectively obliges the consumer to buy the next game like a crew 2, which in turn costs around EUR 50a 80. This gives big businesses too much power and I think that if we go hand in hand in 1 as the EU, we need a better law to make it fairer for consumers. Issue: Reduces business power by prohibiting them from simply depriving you of games while you have paid money for them. The argument of “but then we need to maintain servers and this cost a lot of money” is therefore not what I want to hear and what I am working for. The player should be given the opportunity to start prive servers in what is still the game. So no more of the big company is asked for the game not to be permanently removed from your game of bilothesis. Hopefully, this is a bit clear and you can do something about it. Yours faithfully, Jarno van Rhenen
